name: 6 Day Ijen, Papuma Beach, Tumpak Sewu, Bromo & Malang City Discovery from Surabaya
description: 6-day private tour from Surabaya: Ijen Blue Fire crater (midnight hike), Papuma Beach coastal stop, Tumpak Sewu waterfall canyon descent, Mount Bromo sunrise (private 4WD jeep), and Malang city and Batu highland exploration. Health screening coordinated for Ijen crater access under BBKSDA SE.1658/KSA.9/2024. Fully private — your vehicle, your crew, your group only. Licensed operator NIB 1102230032918.

item.name: Day 1 — Surabaya to Bondowoso for Ijen Staging
item.description: Private transfer from Surabaya to Bondowoso. Check-in at Riverside Homestay, dinner, and mandatory Ijen health screening coordinated by licensed medical staff (SpO2, blood pressure, heart rate). QR-coded certificate required for BBKSDA crater access gate.

item.name: Day 2 — Ijen Crater and Transfer to the Papuma Coastal Side
item.description: Midnight departure to Paltuding base. Guided ascent to Kawah Ijen crater rim (2,386 m). Blue Fire observation in the pre-dawn window. Descent, breakfast, then private transfer south toward the Papuma coastal area. Check-in at Doho Homestay.

item.name: Day 3 — Papuma Beach and Transfer Toward Tumpak Sewu
item.description: Morning at Papuma Beach — dramatic coastal cliffs and Indian Ocean views. Transfer inland toward the Tumpak Sewu staging area. Check-in at Joglo Kecombrang Bromo staging area.

item.name: Day 4 — Tumpak Sewu Canyon Descent and Transfer Toward Bromo
item.description: Guided canyon descent to the base of Tumpak Sewu waterfall — East Java's most spectacular multi-tiered cascade. Transfer north to the Bromo staging area. Check-in at Joglo Kecombrang Bromo.

item.name: Day 5 — Bromo Sunrise and Continue to Malang
item.description: 02:00 departure for Penanjakan viewpoint. Bromo stargazing and sunrise over the Tengger caldera. Private 4WD jeep to the crater base. Transfer to Malang. Check-in at Whiz Prime Malang.

item.name: Day 6 — Malang, Batu, and Surabaya Departure Handoff
item.description: Malang city sights and Batu highland area exploration. Transfer to Surabaya with hotel handoff at Holiday Inn Express Surabaya and private airport transfer.

subjectOf.name: Kawah Ijen
subjectOf.description: Active stratovolcano with the world's largest acidic crater lake and the world's largest visible blue fire phenomenon. Elevation 2,386 m.

subjectOf.name: Papuma Beach
subjectOf.description: Scenic coastal headland in Jember with dramatic cliffs, white sand coves, and Indian Ocean vistas. One of East Java's most photogenic beach stops.

subjectOf.name: Tumpak Sewu Waterfall
subjectOf.description: East Java's most spectacular multi-tiered waterfall, accessible by a guided canyon descent through narrow gorge passages. Among the tallest waterfalls in Indonesia.

subjectOf.name: Mount Bromo
subjectOf.description: Active volcano in the Tengger Semeru National Park. Penanjakan sunrise viewpoint and crater base accessed by private 4WD jeep. Elevation 2,329 m.

subjectOf.name: Malang
subjectOf.description: Colonial highland city in East Java, gateway to the Batu volcanic plateau. Known for Dutch-era architecture, cool climate, and proximity to Semeru National Park.

offers.description: Per-person: IDR 6,050,000 (2 pax) · IDR 5,650,000 (3) · IDR 5,250,000 (4–5) · IDR 5,050,000 (6–7) · IDR 4,850,000 (8–10) · IDR 4,750,000 (11+). Minimum 2 guests. All-inclusive: private transport, crew, 5 nights accommodation + breakfast, entrance fees, 4WD jeep, gas masks + trekking poles, health screening, mineral water, T-shirt.

mainEntity.name: What is included in the 6 Day Ijen, Papuma Beach, Tumpak Sewu, Bromo & Malang City Discovery tour price?

acceptedAnswer.text: All JVTO packages include: private transport (AC MPV for 1–3 guests, Toyota Hiace for 4–9), dedicated crew, all entrance fees and permits, mineral water daily, full pick-up to drop-off assistance, and a JVTO travel T-shirt. This tour additionally includes 5 nights accommodation + breakfast, a private 4WD jeep for Mount Bromo, gas masks and trekking poles for Ijen, and health-screening coordination as required by BBKSDA regulations. International flights, travel insurance, and personal expenses are not included.

mainEntity.name: How does the Ijen health screening work?

acceptedAnswer.text: For tours that include the Ijen night hike, JVTO arranges health-screening coordination — conducted by licensed medical staff — as required under BBKSDA access regulations for the Ijen crater zone (Surat Edaran SE.1658/KSA.9/2024). Vitals measured digitally include SpO2, blood pressure, heart rate, and respiratory history. The certificate carries a QR code verified by BBKSDA at the crater access gate. No valid QR code means no access to the crater zone. Screening is coordinated the evening of Day 1 in Bondowoso.

mainEntity.name: What happens if I do not pass the Ijen health screening?

acceptedAnswer.text: If screening staff or the JVTO team advise that you should not attempt the hike, JVTO follows that decision — it protects your safety and others on the trail. The climb is cancelled for that individual (not the group); alternatives include waiting at base camp or a safe zone. The screening session cost is non-refundable. The group continues.

mainEntity.name: What happens if Bromo or Ijen is closed on my tour dates?

acceptedAnswer.text: JVTO provides a written Plan B briefed before departure, not improvised at the gate. If a site closes due to volcanic activity or park authority order, an alternative route is offered. Guests may rebook at no penalty if the primary sites are inaccessible on the booked dates due to official closure. JVTO monitors MAGMA Indonesia / PVMBG and BBKSDA advisories daily and contacts guests proactively if closures are imminent.

mainEntity.name: Is this a private tour or a shared group tour?

acceptedAnswer.text: 100% private. Your booking covers your own vehicle, your own driver-guide (or driver + escort guide for larger groups), and your own itinerary. No strangers are added to your tour at any point. JVTO does not operate shared group tours.
